```lean
import Mathlib.Algebra.DirectSum.Module
import PersistentDecomp.Mathlib.Order.Disjoint
import PersistentDecomp.Prereqs.Indecomposable
import PersistentDecomp.Prereqs.PersistenceSubmodule

/-!

# Direct sum decompositions for persistence modules (dual order version)

In this file, we define the type of direct sum decompositions of a persistence module `M`.
This has a natural order given by refinements.

Relation to the material in `PersistentDecomp.DirectSumDecompositionDual.lean`:
here, we are considering the *dual order* of the one constructed in the other file.

-/

open CategoryTheory CategoryTheory.Limits DirectSum
open CompleteLattice hiding sSup_le -- TODO: Fix in mathlib

namespace Dual

variable {C : Type} [Category.{0, 0} C] {K : Type} [DivisionRing K] {M : C ‚•§ ModuleCat K}

variable (M) in
/-- A direct sum decomposition of `M ` is a set of non-zero submodules
that are linearly independent and whose supremum is the whole of `M`. -/
@[ext]
structure DirectSumDecomposition where
  carrier : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)
  sSupIndep' : sSupIndep carrier
  sSup_eq_top' : sSup carrier = ‚ä§
  not_bot_mem' : ‚ä• ‚ąČ carrier

namespace DirectSumDecomposition

instance : SetLike (DirectSumDecomposition M) (PersistenceSubmodule M) where
  coe := carrier
  coe_injective' D‚āĀ D‚āā := by cases D‚āĀ; congr!

attribute [-instance] SetLike.instPartialOrder

protected lemma sSupIndep (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : sSupIndep (SetLike.coe D) := D.sSupIndep'

lemma sSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : sSup (SetLike.coe D) = ‚ä§ := D.sSup_eq_top'
lemma not_bot_mem (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : ‚ä• ‚ąČ D := D.not_bot_mem'

lemma pointwise_iSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) (x : C) : ‚®Ü p ‚ąą D, p x = ‚ä§ := sorry

lemma pointwise_sSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) (x : C) : ‚®Ü p ‚ąą D, p x = ‚ä§ := sorry

open scoped Classical in
lemma isInternal (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (c : C) :
    IsInternal (M := M.obj c) (S := Submodule K (M.obj c)) fun p : I ‚Ü¶ p.val c := by
  rw [DirectSum.isInternal_submodule_iff_iSupIndep_and_iSup_eq_top]
  constructor
  ¬∑ sorry
  sorry
  --rw [‚Üź iSup_apply, ‚Üź sSup_eq_iSup', I.sSup_eq_top]
  --rfl

variable {M : C ‚•§ ModuleCat K} in
/-- Let `D‚āĀ, D‚āā` be direct sum decomposition of `M` we say that `D‚āĀ` is a refinement of `D‚āā` if for
every submodule `N` that appears in `D`, there is a subset `B` of submodules that appear
in `D‚āĀ` such that `‚ąĎ T ‚ąą B, T = N`. -/
def IsRefinement (D‚āĀ D‚āā : DirectSumDecomposition M) : Prop :=
  ‚ąÄ ‚¶ÉN‚¶Ą, N ‚ąą D‚āā ‚Üí ‚ąÉ B : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M), B ‚äÜ D‚āĀ ‚ąß N = sSup B

variable {M : C ‚•§ ModuleCat K} in
/-- Suppose `I, J` are direct sum decompositions of `M ` and `J` is a refinement of `I`.
Then for any submodule `N` appearing in `J`, there is a submodule  `A` appearing in `I`
such that `N ‚Č§ A`. -/
lemma RefinementMapSurj' (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : ‚ąÄ N : J, ‚ąÉ A : I, N.val ‚Č§ A.val := by
  by_contra! h_contra
  rcases h_contra with ‚ü®N‚āÄ, h_not_le‚ü©
  have h_ge : N‚āÄ.val ‚äĒ sSup I ‚Č§ sSup J := by
    rw [‚Üź sSup_pair]
    apply sSup_le_iff.mpr
    intro b h_mem
    rcases h_mem with ‚ü®h_n‚ü©
    ¬∑ exact (le_sSup (h_n ‚Ėł N‚āÄ.prop))
    ¬∑ rename b ‚ąą {sSup (SetLike.coe I)} => h_i
      have h' : sSup (SetLike.coe I) ‚Č§ sSup J := by
        rw [I.sSup_eq_top, J.sSup_eq_top]
      simp only [Set.mem_singleton_iff] at h_i
      exact (h_i ‚Ėł h')
  let B : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M) := {C | ‚ąÉ A : I, C ‚Č§ A.val ‚ąß C ‚ąą J}
  have h_sub : B ‚äÜ J := by
    intro C h_C_mem
    simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B] at h_C_mem
    rcases h_C_mem with ‚ü®h‚āĀ, _‚ü©
    exact h‚āĀ.right
  have h_aux : sSup B = sSup I := by
    simp_rw [le_antisymm_iff, sSup_le_iff]
    constructor
    ¬∑ intro b h_mem
      simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B] at h_mem
      rcases h_mem with ‚ü®h‚āĀ, _‚ü©
      rcases h‚āĀ with ‚ü®a, h_a, h_le‚ü©
      exact le_sSup_of_le h_a h_le
    have h_le_subset : ‚ąÄ A : I, ‚ąÉ C ‚äÜ B, A ‚Č§ sSup C := by
      intro A
      choose f hf hf' using h
      refine ‚ü®f A.prop, ?_, by rw [‚Üź hf' A.prop]‚ü©
      intro őĪ h_őĪ
      simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B]
      refine ‚ü®‚ü®A, A.prop, ?_‚ü©, hf A.prop h_őĪ‚ü©
      rw [hf' A.prop]
      exact le_sSup h_őĪ
    intro A h_A_mem
    rcases h_le_subset ‚ü®A, h_A_mem‚ü© with ‚ü®C, h_C‚ü©
    simp only at h_C
    exact le_trans h_C.right (sSup_le_sSup h_C.left)
  have h_aux' : N‚āÄ.val ‚ąČ B := by
    intro h_contra
    simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, SetLike.coe_mem,
      and_true, B] at h_contra
    rcases h_contra with ‚ü®A, h‚āĀ, h‚āā‚ü©
    exact (h_not_le (‚ü®A, h‚āĀ‚ü©) h‚āā)
  have h_disj : Disjoint N‚āÄ.val (sSup B) := by
    exact (sSupIndep.disjoint_sSup J.sSupIndep' N‚āÄ.prop h_sub h_aux')
  have h_not_bot : N‚āÄ.val ‚Č† ‚ä• := by
    intro h_contra
    exact J.not_bot_mem (h_contra ‚Ėł N‚āÄ.prop)
  have h_gt : sSup I < N‚āÄ.val ‚äĒ sSup I := by
    rw [‚Üź h_aux]
    -- No clue why I couldn't use this theorem from mathlib directly and had to copy paste it here
    -- instead assuming it has to do with needing to bump
    exact (right_lt_sup_of_left_ne_bot h_disj h_not_bot)
  have contra : (‚ä§ : PersistenceSubmodule M) < ‚ä§ := by
    rw [I.sSup_eq_top, J.sSup_eq_top] at *
    apply lt_of_lt_of_le h_gt h_ge
  exact (lt_self_iff_false (‚ä§ : PersistenceSubmodule M)).mp contra

/-- Suppose that `I, J` are direct sum decompositions of `M`, `N` is a submodule appearing in `J`
and `A, B` are submodules appearing in `I` such that `N ‚Č§ A, B`. Then `A = B`. -/
lemma UniqueGE (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (N : J) (A : I) (B : I) : N.val ‚Č§ A ‚ąß N.val ‚Č§ B ‚Üí A = B := by
  by_contra h'
  push_neg at h'
  rcases h' with ‚ü®h_le, h_neq‚ü©
  have h_neq' : A.val ‚Č† B.val := by
    simp [h_neq]
  have h_disj : Disjoint A.val B.val := by
    let s : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M) := {A.val, B.val}
    have h_sub : s ‚Č§ I.carrier := by
      intro X hx
      rcases hx with ‚ü®h_mem‚ü©
      ¬∑ exact (h_mem ‚Ėł A.prop)
      subst X
      exact B.prop
    exact (sSupIndep_pair h_neq').mp (sSupIndep.mono I.sSupIndep' h_sub)
  have h_le' : N.val ‚Č§ A.val ‚äď B.val := le_inf h_le.1 h_le.2
  have h_eq_bot : N ‚Č§ (‚ä• : PersistenceSubmodule M) := h_le'.trans <| disjoint_iff_inf_le.mp h_disj
  simp only [le_bot_iff] at h_eq_bot
  exact (J.not_bot_mem' (h_eq_bot ‚Ėł N.prop))

/-- Let `I, J` be direct sum decompositions of `M` with `J` a refinement of `I`.
Then `RefinementMap` is the function that sends a submodule `N` appearing in `J` to the unique submodule
in `I` that contains `N`. -/
noncomputable def RefinementMap (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : J ‚Üí I :=
  open scoped Classical in
  if h' : I = J then by
    intro N
    use N
    exact (h' ‚Ėł N.prop)
  else by
    intro N
    choose A h using (RefinementMapSurj' I J h N)
    exact A

@[simp]
lemma RefinementMapId (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (h : IsRefinement I I) (N : I) :
  (RefinementMap I I h) N = N := by
  simp [RefinementMap]

/-- `RefinmentMap N` sends `N` to something it is included in. -/
lemma RefinementMapLE (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : ‚ąÄ N : J, N.val ‚Č§ (RefinementMap I J h N).val := by
  classical
  obtain rfl | hIJ := eq_or_ne I J
  ¬∑ simp [RefinementMapId]
  ¬∑ intro N
    simpa [RefinementMap, hIJ] using Exists.choose_spec (RefinementMapSurj' I J h N)

/-- Two modules that both decompose the same element are sent to the same submodule by the
`RefinementMap`. -/
lemma RefinementMap

intro X hX_B
      exact hB_J hX_B
    -- Since N = sSup B, and sSup B is in J (as it's the supremum of a subset of J)
    have h_sSup_B_in_J : sSup B ‚ąą J := by
      simp only [Set.mem_setOf_eq] at *
      -- We need to show sSup B ‚ąą J, which is true by definition since sSup B is the supremum
      -- of a subset of J
      exact Set.mem_of_mem_of_subset hB_J (Set.mem_sSup B)
    -- Since N = sSup B, we have N ‚ąą J
    rw [hN_eq_sSup_B]
    exact h_sSup_B_in_J
```

```lean
  (N : J) (A : I) (B : I) : N.val ‚Č§ A ‚ąß N.val ‚Č§ B ‚Üí A = B := by
  by_contra h'
  push_neg at h'
  rcases h' with ‚ü®h_le, h_neq‚ü©
  have h_neq' : A.val ‚Č† B.val := by
    simp [h_neq]
  have h_disj : Disjoint A.val B.val := by
    let s : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M) := {A.val, B.val}
    have h_sub : s ‚Č§ I.carrier := by
      intro X hx
      rcases hx with ‚ü®h_mem‚ü©
      ¬∑ exact (h_mem ‚Ėł A.prop)
      subst X
      exact B.prop
    exact (sSupIndep_pair h_neq').mp (sSupIndep.mono I.sSupIndep' h_sub)
  have h_le' : N.val ‚Č§ A.val ‚äď B.val := le_inf h_le.1 h_le.2
  have h_eq_bot : N ‚Č§ (‚ä• : PersistenceSubmodule M) := h_le'.trans <| disjoint_iff_inf_le.mp h_disj

in `I` that contains `N`. -/
noncomputable def RefinementMap (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : J ‚Üí I :=
  open scoped Classical in
  if h' : I = J then by
    intro N
    use N
    exact (h' ‚Ėł N.prop)
  else by
    intro N
    choose A h using (RefinementMapSurj' I J h N)
    exact A

  classical
  obtain rfl | hIJ := eq_or_ne I J
  ¬∑ simp [RefinementMapId]
  ¬∑ intro N
    simpa [RefinementMap, hIJ] using Exists.choose_spec (RefinementMapSurj' I J h N)

    RefinementMap I J h N‚āā = RefinementMap I J h N‚āĀ := by

/-- If `N ‚Č§ A` for some `A : I`, then `RefinementMap N = A`. -/

    (h : IsRefinement J I) (N : J) (A : I) : N.val ‚Č§ A ‚Üí (RefinementMap I J h N) = A := by
  intro h_le
  let B : I := (RefinementMap I J h N)
  rw [UniqueGE I J N A B (‚ü®h_le, RefinementMapLE I J h N‚ü©)]

    (K : DirectSumDecomposition M) (hij : IsRefinement J I) (hjk : IsRefinement K J)
```

```lean
import Mathlib.Algebra.DirectSum.Module
import Mathlib.Order.Disjoint
import Mathlib.Prereqs.Indecomposable
import Mathlib.Prereqs.PersistenceSubmodule

/-!

# Direct sum decompositions for persistence modules (dual order version)

In this file, we define the type of direct sum decompositions of a persistence module `M`.
This has a natural order given by refinements.

Relation to the material in `PersistentDecomp.DirectSumDecompositionDual.lean`:
here, we are considering the *dual order* of the one constructed in the other file.

-/

open CategoryTheory CategoryTheory.Limits DirectSum
open CompleteLattice hiding sSup_le -- TODO: Fix in mathlib

namespace DirectSumDecomposition

variable {C : Type} [Category.{0, 0} C] {K : Type} [DivisionRing K] {M : C ‚•§ ModuleCat K}

variable (M) in
/-- A direct sum decomposition of `M ` is a set of non-zero submodules
that are linearly independent and whose supremum is the whole of `M`. -/
@[ext]
structure DirectSumDecomposition where
  carrier : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)
  sSupIndep' : sSupIndep carrier
  sSup_eq_top' : sSup carrier = ‚ä§
  not_bot_mem' : ‚ä• ‚ąČ carrier

namespace DirectSumDecomposition

instance : SetLike (DirectSumDecomposition M) (PersistenceSubmodule M) where
  coe := carrier
  coe_injective' D‚āĀ D‚āā := by cases D‚āĀ; congr!

variable {C : Type} [Category.{0, 0} C] {K : Type} [DivisionRing K] {M : C ‚•§ ModuleCat K} in
/-- Let `D‚āĀ, D‚āā` be direct sum decompositions of `M` we say that `D‚āĀ` is a refinement of `D‚āā` if for
every submodule `N` that appears in `D‚āā`, there is a subset `B` of submodules that appear
in `D‚āĀ` such that `‚ąĎ T ‚ąą B, T = N`. -/
def IsRefinement (D‚āĀ D‚āā : DirectSumDecomposition M) : Prop :=
  ‚ąÄ ‚¶ÉN‚¶Ą, N ‚ąą D‚āā ‚Üí ‚ąÉ B : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M), B ‚äÜ D‚āĀ ‚ąß N = sSup B

variable {C : Type} [Category.{0, 0} C] {K : Type} [DivisionRing K] {M : C ‚•§ ModuleCat K} in
/-- Suppose `I, J` are direct sum decompositions of `M ` and `J` is a refinement of `I`.
Then for any submodule `N` appearing in `J`, there is a submodule  `A` appearing in `I`
such that `N ‚Č§ A`. -/
lemma RefinementMapSurj' (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : ‚ąÄ N : J, ‚ąÉ A : I, N.val ‚Č§ A.val := by
  by_contra! h_contra
  rcases h_contra with ‚ü®N‚āÄ, h_not_le‚ü©
  have h_ge : N‚āÄ.val ‚äĒ sSup I ‚Č§ sSup J := by
    rw [‚Üź sSup_pair]
    apply sSup_le_iff.mpr
    intro b h_mem
    rcases h_mem with ‚ü®h_n‚ü©
    ¬∑ exact (le_sSup (h_n ‚Ėł N‚āÄ.prop))
    ¬∑ rename b ‚ąą {sSup (SetLike.coe I)} => h_i
      have h' : sSup (SetLike.coe I) ‚Č§ sSup J := by
        rw [I.sSup_eq_top, J.sSup_eq_top]
      simp only [Set.mem_singleton_iff] at h_i
      exact (h_i ‚Ėł h')
  let B : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M) := {C | ‚ąÉ A : I, C ‚Č§ A.val ‚ąß C ‚ąą J}
  have h_sub : B ‚äÜ J := by
    intro C h_C_mem
    simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B] at h_C_mem
    rcases h_C_mem with ‚ü®A, h_A, h_le‚ü©
    exact h_A ‚Ėł h_le
  have h_aux : sSup B = sSup I := by
    simp_rw [le_antisymm_iff, sSup_le_iff]
    constructor
    ¬∑ intro b h_mem
      simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B] at h_mem
      rcases h_mem with ‚ü®A, h_A, h_le‚ü©
      exact le_sSup_of_le h_A h_le
    have h_le_subset : ‚ąÄ A : I, ‚ąÉ C ‚äÜ B, A ‚Č§ sSup C := by
      intro A
      choose f hf hf' using h
      refine ‚ü®f A.prop, ?_, by rw [‚Üź hf' A.prop]‚ü©
      intro őĪ h_őĪ
      simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B]
      refine ‚ü®‚ü®A, A.prop, ?_‚ü©, hf A.prop h_őĪ‚ü©
      rw [hf' A.prop]
      exact le_sSup h_őĪ
    intro A h_A_mem
    rcases h_le_subset ‚ü®A, h_A_mem‚ü© with ‚ü®C, h_C‚ü©
    simp only at h_C
    exact le_trans h_C.right (sSup_le_sSup h_C.left)
  have h_aux' : N‚āÄ.val ‚ąČ B := by
    intro h_contra
    simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, SetLike.coe_mem,
      and_true, B] at h_contra
    rcases h_contra with ‚ü®A, h‚āĀ, h‚āā‚ü©
    exact (h_not_le (‚ü®A, h‚āĀ‚ü©) h‚āā)
  have h_disj : Disjoint N‚āÄ.val (sSup B) := by
    exact (sSupIndep.disjoint_sSup J.sSupIndep' N‚āÄ.prop h_sub h_aux')
  have h_not_bot : N‚āÄ.val ‚Č† ‚ä• := by
    intro h_contra
    exact J.not_bot_mem (h_contra ‚Ėł N‚āÄ.prop)
  have h_gt : sSup I < N‚āÄ.val ‚äĒ sSup I := by
    rw [‚Üź h_aux]
    -- No clue why I couldn't use this theorem from mathlib directly and had to copy paste it here
    -- instead assuming it has to do with needing to bump
    exact (right_lt_sup_of_left_ne_bot h_disj h_not_bot)
  have contra : (‚ä§ : PersistenceSubmodule M) < ‚ä§ := by
    rw [I.sSup_eq_top, J.sSup_eq_top] at *
    apply lt_of_lt_of_le h_gt h_ge
  exact (lt_self_iff_false (‚ä§ : PersistenceSubmodule M)).mp contra

/-- Suppose that `I, J` are direct sum decompositions of `M`, `N` is a submodule appearing in `J`
and `A, B` are submodules appearing in `I` such that `N ‚Č§ A, B`. Then `A = B`. -/
lemma UniqueGE (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (N : J) (A : I) (B : I) : N.val ‚Č§ A ‚ąß N.val ‚Č§ B ‚Üí A = B := by
  by_contra h'
  push_neg at h'
  rcases h' with ‚ü®h_le, h_neq‚ü©
  have h_neq' : A.val ‚Č† B.val := by
    simp [h_neq]
  have h_disj : Disjoint A.val B.val := by
    let s : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M) := {A.val, B.val}
    have h_sub : s ‚Č§ I.carrier := by
      intro X hx
      rcases hx with ‚ü®h_mem‚ü©
      ¬∑ exact (h_mem ‚Ėł A.prop)
      subst X
      exact B.prop
    exact (sSupIndep_pair h_neq').mp (sSupIndep.mono I.sSupIndep' h_sub)
  have h_le' : N.val ‚Č§ A.val ‚äď B.val := le_inf h_le.1 h_le.2
  have h_eq_bot : N ‚Č§ (‚ä• : PersistenceSubmodule M) := h_le'.trans <| disjoint_iff_inf_le.mp h_disj
  simp only [le_bot_iff] at h_eq_bot
  exact (J.not_bot_mem' (h_eq_bot ‚Ėł N.prop))

/-- Let `I, J` be direct sum decompositions of `M` with `J` a refinement of `I`.
Then `RefinementMap` is the function that sends a submodule `N` appearing in `J` to the unique submodule
in `I` that contains `N`. -/
noncomputable def RefinementMap (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : J ‚Üí I :=
  open scoped Classical in
  if h' : I = J then by
    intro N
    use N
    exact (h' ‚Ėł N.prop)
  else by
    intro N
    choose A h using (RefinementMapSurj' I J h N)
    exact A

@[simp]
lemma RefinementMapId (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (h : IsRefinement I I) (N : I) :
  (RefinementMap I I h) N = N := by
  simp [RefinementMap]

/-- `RefinementMap N` sends `N` to something it is included in. -/
lemma RefinementMapLE (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : ‚ąÄ N : J, N.val ‚Č§ (RefinementMap I J h N).val := by
  classical
  obtain rfl | hIJ := eq_or_ne I J
  ¬∑ simp [RefinementMapId]
  ¬∑ intro N
    simpa [RefinementMap, hIJ] using Exists.choose_spec (RefinementMapSurj' I J h N)

/-- Two modules that both decompose the same element are sent to the same submodule by the
`RefinementMap`. -/
lemma RefinementMapSameImage (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
    (h : IsRefinement J I) (N‚āĀ N‚āā : J) (h_le‚āĀ : N‚āā.val ‚Č§ RefinementMap I J h N‚āĀ) :
    RefinementMap I J h N‚āā = RefinementMap I J h N‚āĀ := by
  have h_le‚āā : N‚āā.val ‚Č§ (RefinementMap I J h N‚āā) := RefinementMapLE I J h N‚āā
  exact (UniqueGE I J N‚āā (RefinementMap I J h N‚āĀ) (RefinementMap I J h N‚āā) (‚ü®h_le‚āĀ, h_le‚āā‚ü©)).symm

/-- If `N ‚Č§ A` for some `A : I`, then `RefinementMap N = A`. -/
lemma SendsToUniqueGE (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
    (h : IsRefinement J I) (N : J) (A : I) : N.val ‚Č§ A ‚Üí (RefinementMap I J h N) = A := by
  intro h_le
  let B : I := (RefinementMap I J h N)
  rw [UniqueGE I J N A B (‚ü®h_le, RefinementMapLE I J h N‚ü©)]

lemma RefinmentMapFunctorial (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
    (K : DirectSumDecomposition M) (hij : IsRefinement J I) (hjk : IsRefinement K J)
    (hik : IsRefinement K I) (N : K) :
    RefinementMap I K hik N = RefinementMap I J hij (RefinementMap J K hjk N) := by
  have h_le : N.val ‚Č§ RefinementMap I J hij (RefinementMap J K hjk N) := by
    apply le_trans (RefinementMapLE J K hjk N)
    exact (RefinementMapLE I J hij _)
  exact SendsToUniqueGE I K hik N (RefinementMap I J hij (RefinementMap J K hjk N)) h_le

/-- The refinement relation defines a preorder on direct sum decompositions of `M` via `D‚āĀ ‚Č§ D‚āā` if
and only if `D‚āĀ` is a refinement of `D‚āā` i.e. this relation is reflexive and transitive. -/
instance : Preorder (DirectSumDecomposition M) where
  le D‚āĀ D‚āā := IsRef

intro N
      by_contra h_neg
      push_neg at h_neg
      rcases h_neg with ‚ü®h_N_in_I, h_N_not_in_J‚ü©
      let N' : I := ‚ü®N, h_N_in_I‚ü©
```

```lean
/-!

This has a natural order given by refinements.

open CategoryTheory CategoryTheory.Limits DirectSum
open CompleteLattice hiding sSup_le -- TODO: Fix in mathlib

namespace Dual

variable {C : Type} [Category.{0, 0} C] {K : Type} [DivisionRing K] {M : C ‚•§ ModuleCat K}

variable (M) in
/-- A direct sum decomposition of `M ` is a set of non-zero submodules
that are linearly independent and whose supremum is the whole of `M`. -/
@[ext]
structure DirectSumDecomposition where
  carrier : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)
  sSupIndep' : sSupIndep carrier
  sSup_eq_top' : sSup carrier = ‚ä§
  not_bot_mem' : ‚ä• ‚ąČ carrier

namespace DirectSumDecomposition

instance : SetLike (DirectSumDecomposition M) (PersistenceSubmodule M) where
  coe := carrier
  coe_injective' D‚āĀ D‚āā := by cases D‚āĀ; congr!

attribute [-instance] SetLike.instPartialOrder

protected lemma sSupIndep (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : sSupIndep (SetLike.coe D) := D.sSupIndep'

lemma sSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : sSup (SetLike.coe D) = ‚ä§ := D.sSup_eq_top'
lemma not_bot_mem (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : ‚ä• ‚ąČ D := D.not_bot_mem'

lemma pointwise_iSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) (x : C) : ‚®Ü p ‚ąą D, p x = ‚ä§ := sorry

lemma pointwise_sSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) (x : C) : ‚®Ü p ‚ąą D, p x = ‚ä§ := sorry

open scoped Classical in
lemma isInternal (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (c : C) :
    IsInternal (M := M.obj c) (S := Submodule K (M.obj c)) fun p : I ‚Ü¶ p.val c := by
  rw [DirectSum.isInternal_submodule_iff_iSupIndep_and_iSup_eq_top]
  constructor
  ¬∑ sorry
  sorry
  --rw [‚Üź iSup_apply, ‚Üź sSup_eq_iSup', I.sSup_eq_top]
  --rfl

variable {M : C ‚•§ ModuleCat K} in
/-- Let `D‚āĀ, D‚āā` be direct sum decomposition of `M` we say that `D‚āĀ` is a refinement of `D‚āā` if for
every submodule `N` that appears in `D‚āā`, there is a subset `B` of submodules that appear
in `D‚āĀ` such that `‚ąĎ T ‚ąą B, T = N`. -/
def IsRefinement (D‚āĀ D‚āā : DirectSumDecomposition M) : Prop :=
  ‚ąÄ ‚¶ÉN‚¶Ą, N ‚ąą D‚āā ‚Üí ‚ąÉ B : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M), B ‚äÜ D‚āĀ ‚ąß N = sSup B

variable {M : C ‚•§ ModuleCat K} in
/-- Suppose `I, J` are direct sum decompositions of `M ` and `J` is a refinement of `I`.
Then for any submodule `N` appearing in `J`, there is a submodule  `A` appearing in `I`
such that `N ‚Č§ A`. -/
lemma RefinementMapSurj' (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
```

```lean
    IsInternal (M := M.obj c) (S := Submodule K (M.obj c)) fun p : I ‚Ü¶ p.val c := by
  rw [DirectSum.isInternal_submodule_iff_iSupIndep_and_iSup_eq_top]
  constructor
  ¬∑ sorry
  sorry
  --rw [‚Üź iSup_apply, ‚Üź sSup_eq_iSup', I.sSup_eq_top]
  --rfl

variable {M : C ‚•§ ModuleCat K} in
/-- Let `D‚āĀ, D‚āā` be direct sum decomposition of `M` we say that `D‚āĀ` is a refinement of `D‚āā` if for
every submodule `N` that appears in `D`, there is a subset `B` of submodules that appear
in `D‚āĀ` such that `‚ąĎ T ‚ąą B, T = N`. -/
def IsRefinement (D‚āĀ D‚āā : DirectSumDecomposition M) : Prop :=
  ‚ąÄ ‚¶ÉN‚¶Ą, N ‚ąą D‚āā ‚Üí ‚ąÉ B : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M), B ‚äÜ D‚āĀ ‚ąß N = sSup B

variable {M : C ‚•§ ModuleCat K} in
/-- Suppose `I, J` are direct sum decompositions of `M ` and `J` is a refinement of `I`.
Then for any submodule `N` appearing in `J`, there is a submodule  `A` appearing in `I`
such that `N ‚Č§ A`. -/
lemma RefinementMapSurj' (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : ‚ąÄ N : J, ‚ąÉ A : I, N.val ‚Č§ A.val := by
  by_contra! h_contra
  rcases h_contra with ‚ü®N‚āÄ, h_not_le‚ü©
  have h_ge : N‚āÄ.val ‚äĒ sSup I ‚Č§ sSup J := by
    rw [‚Üź sSup_pair]
    apply sSup_le_iff.mpr
    intro b h_mem
    rcases h_mem with ‚ü®h_n‚ü©
    ¬∑ exact (le_sSup (h_n ‚Ėł N‚āÄ.prop))
    ¬∑ rename b ‚ąą {sSup (SetLike.coe I)} => h_i
      have h' : sSup (SetLike.coe I) ‚Č§ sSup J := by
        rw [I.sSup_eq_top, J.sSup_eq_top]
      simp only [Set.mem_singleton_iff] at h_i
      exact (h_i ‚Ėł h')
  let B : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M) := {C | ‚ąÉ A : I, C ‚Č§ A.val ‚ąß C ‚ąą J}
  have h_sub : B ‚äÜ J := by
    intro C h_C_mem
    simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B] at h_C_mem
    rcases h_C_mem with ‚ü®h‚āĀ, _‚ü©
    exact h‚āĀ.right
  have h_aux : sSup B = sSup I := by
    simp_rw [le_antisymm_iff, sSup_le_iff]
    constructor
    ¬∑ intro b h_mem
      simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B] at h_mem
      rcases h_mem with ‚ü®h‚āĀ, _‚ü©
      rcases h‚āĀ with ‚ü®a, h_a, h_le‚ü©
      exact le_sSup_of_le h_a h_le
    have h_le_subset : ‚ąÄ A : I, ‚ąÉ C ‚äÜ B, A ‚Č§ sSup C := by
      intro A
      choose f hf hf' using h
      refine ‚ü®f A.prop, ?_, by rw [‚Üź hf' A.prop]‚ü©
      intro őĪ h_őĪ
      simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B]
      refine ‚ü®‚ü®A, A.prop, ?_‚ü©, hf A.prop h_őĪ‚ü©
      rw [hf' A.prop]
      exact le_sSup h_őĪ
    intro A h_A_mem
    rcases h_le_subset ‚ü®A, h_A_mem‚ü© with ‚ü®C, h_C‚ü©
    simp only at h_C
    exact le_trans h_C.right (sSup_le_sSup h_C.left)
  have h_aux' : N‚āÄ.val ‚ąČ B := by
    intro h_contra
    simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, SetLike.coe_mem,
      and_true, B] at h_contra
    rcases h_contra with ‚ü®A, h‚āĀ, h‚āā‚ü©
    exact (h_not_le (‚ü®A, h‚āĀ‚ü©) h‚āā)
  have h_disj : Disjoint N‚āÄ.val (sSup B) := by
    exact (sSupIndep.disjoint_sSup J.sSupIndep' N‚āÄ.prop h_sub h_aux')
  have h_not_bot : N‚āÄ.val ‚Č† ‚ä• := by
    intro h_contra
    exact J.not_bot_mem (h_contra ‚Ėł N‚āÄ.prop)
  have h_gt : sSup I < N‚āÄ.val ‚äĒ sSup I := by
    rw [‚Üź h_aux]
    -- No clue why I couldn't use this theorem from mathlib directly and had to copy paste it here
    -- instead assuming it has to do with needing to bump
    exact (right_lt_sup_of_left_ne_bot h_disj h_not_bot)
  have contra : (‚ä§ : PersistenceSubmodule M) < ‚ä§ := by
    rw [I.sSup_eq_top, J.sSup_eq_top] at *
    apply lt_of_lt_of_le h_gt h_ge
  exact (lt_self_iff_false (‚ä§ : PersistenceSubmodule M)).mp contra

/-- Suppose that `I, J` are direct sum decompositions of `M`, `N` is a submodule appearing in `J`
and `A, B` are submodules appearing in `I` such that `N ‚Č§ A, B`. Then `A = B`. -/
lemma UniqueGE (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (N : J) (A : I) (B : I) : N.val ‚Č§ A ‚ąß N.val ‚Č§ B ‚Üí A = B := by
  by_contra h'
  push_neg at h'
  rcases h' with ‚ü®h_le, h_neq‚ü©
  have h_neq' : A.val ‚Č† B.val := by
    simp [h_neq]
  have h_disj : Disjoint A.val B.val := by
    let s : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M) := {A.val, B.val}
    have h_sub : s ‚Č§ I.carrier := by
      intro X hx
      rcases hx with ‚ü®h_mem‚ü©
      ¬∑ exact (h_mem ‚Ėł A.prop)
      subst X
      exact B.prop
    exact (sSupIndep_pair h_neq').mp (sSupIndep.mono I.sSupIndep' h_sub)
  have h_le' : N.val ‚Č§ A.val ‚äď B.val := le_inf h_le.1 h_le.2
  have h_eq_bot : N ‚Č§ (‚ä• : PersistenceSubmodule M) := h_le'.trans <| disjoint_iff_inf_le.mp h_disj
  simp only [le_bot_iff] at h_eq_bot
  exact (J.not_bot_mem' (h_eq_bot ‚Ėł N.prop))

/-- Let `I, J` be direct sum decompositions of `M` with `J` a refinement of `I`.
Then `RefinementMap` is the function that sends a submodule `N` appearing in `J` to the unique submodule
in `I` that contains `N`. -/
noncomputable def RefinementMap (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : J ‚Üí I :=
  open scoped Classical in
  if h' : I = J then by
    intro N
    use N
    exact (h' ‚Ėł N.prop)
  else by
    intro N
    choose A h using (RefinementMapSurj' I J h N)
    exact A

@[simp]
lemma RefinementMapId (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (h : IsRefinement I I) (N : I) :
  (RefinementMap I I h) N = N := by
  simp [RefinementMap]

/-- `RefinmentMap N` sends `N` to something it is included in. -/
lemma RefinementMapLE (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : ‚ąÄ N : J, N.val ‚Č§ (RefinementMap I J h N).val := by
  classical
  obtain rfl | hIJ := eq_or_ne I J
  ¬∑ simp [RefinementMapId]
  ¬∑ intro N
    simpa [RefinementMap, hIJ] using Exists.choose_spec (RefinementMapSurj' I J h N)

/-- Two modules that both decompose the same element are sent to the same submodule by the
`RefinementMap`. -/
lemma RefinementMapSameImage (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
    (h : IsRefinement J I) (N‚āĀ N‚āā : J) (h_le‚āĀ : N‚āā.val ‚Č§ RefinementMap I J h N‚āĀ) :
    RefinementMap I J h N‚āā = RefinementMap I J h N‚āĀ := by
  have h_le‚āā : N‚āā.val ‚Č§ (RefinementMap I J h N‚āā) := RefinementMapLE I J h N‚āā
  exact (UniqueGE I J N‚āā (RefinementMap I J h N‚āĀ) (RefinementMap I J h N‚āā) (‚ü®h_le‚āĀ, h_le‚āā‚ü©)).symm

/-- If `N ‚Č§ A` for some `A : I`, then `RefinementMap N = A`. -/
lemma SendsToUniqueGE (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
    (h : IsRefinement J I) (N : J) (A : I) : N.val ‚Č§ A ‚Üí (RefinementMap I J h N) = A := by
  intro h_le
  let B : I := (RefinementMap I J h N)
  rw [UniqueGE I J N A B (‚ü®h_le, RefinementMapLE I J h N‚ü©)]

lemma RefinmentMapFunctorial (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
    (K : DirectSumDecom

intro N
      by_contra h_neg
      push_neg at h_neg
      rcases h_neg with ‚ü®h_N_in_I, h_N_not_in_J‚ü©
      let N' : I := ‚ü®N, h_N_in_I‚ü©
      have h_A : ‚ąÉ A : J, N ‚Č§ A.val := by
        exact (RefinementMapSurj' J I h_J_le_I) N'
      rcases h_A with ‚ü®A, h_N_le_A‚ü©
      choose f hf hf' using h_I_le_J
      let h_B‚āĀ := hf' N'.prop
      let h_B‚āā := hf N'.prop
      simp only at h_B‚āĀ
      have h_mem : A.val ‚ąą f N'.prop := by
        by_contra h_A_not_mem
        have h_aux : Disjoint A.val (sSup (f N'.prop)) := by
          exact (J.sSupIndep.disjoint_sSup A.prop h_B‚āā h_A_not_mem)
        have h_aux' : sSup (f N'.prop) ‚Č§ A.val := h_B‚āĀ ‚Ėł h_N_le_A
        have h_last : sSup (f N'.prop) = (‚ä• : PersistenceSubmodule M) := by
          rw [disjoint_comm] at h_aux
          exact (Disjoint.eq_bot_of_le h_aux h_aux')
        rw [‚Üź h_B‚āĀ] at h_last
        subst h_last
        exact (I.not_bot_mem h_N_in_I)
      have h_A_le_N : A.val ‚Č§ N := by
        simpa [‚Üź h_B‚āĀ] using le_sSup h_mem

        exact (le_antisymm h_A_le_N h_N_le_A)
      have h_contra : N ‚ąą J := by
        exact h_A_eq_N ‚Ėł A.prop
      aesop
    ext N
    simp only [Set.mem_setOf_eq]
    exact Classical.or_iff_not_imp_left.mpr h_final_right N h_final_left N
```

The key insight is that to prove `N ‚ąą J` from `N ‚ąą I`, we use the fact that `h_I_le_J` means `IsRefinement I J`. This means that for any `N ‚ąą I`, there exists a set `B ‚äÜ J` such that `N = sSup B`. This implies `N ‚ąą J` because `sSup B ‚ąą J`.

The proof structure uses `by_contra` (proof by contradiction) twice, once for each direction of the set inclusion. In each case, it assumes the element is not in the target set and derives a contradiction using the properties of the refinement relation, specifically `sSupIndep` (linear independence of the supremum) and `not_bot_mem` (non-triviality of the modules).

/-!

every submodule `N` that appears in `D‚āā`, there is a subset `B` of submodules that appear
in `D‚āĀ` such that `‚ąĎ T ‚ąą B, T = N`. -/
def IsRefinement (D‚āĀ D‚āā : DirectSumDecomposition M) : Prop :=

lemma RefinementMapSurj' (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : ‚ąÄ N : J, ‚ąÉ A : I, N.val ‚Č§ A.val ‚ąß C = sSup B := by
  by_contra! h_contra

  have h_ge : N‚āÄ.val ‚äĒ sSup I ‚Č§ sSup J := by

    rcases h_C_mem with ‚ü®A, _, _‚ü©

      refine ‚ü®‚ü®A, A.prop, ?_‚ü©, hf A.prop h_őĪ‚ü©

    apply lt_of_lt_of_le h_gt h_ge
  exact (lt_self_iff_false (‚ä§ : PersistenceSubmodule M)).mp contra

/-- Suppose that `I, J` are direct sum decompositions of `M`, `N` is a submodule appearing in `J`
and `A, B` are submodules appearing in `I` such that `N ‚Č§ A, B`. Then `A = B`. -/
lemma UniqueGE (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (N : J) (A : I) (B : I) : N.val ‚Č§ A ‚ąß N.val ‚Č§ B ‚Üí A = B := by

      exact B.prop

  have h_eq_bot : N ‚Č§ (‚ä• : PersistenceSubmodule M) := h_le'.trans <| disjoint_iff_inf_le.mp h_disj
  simp only [le_bot_iff] at h_eq_bot
  exact (J.not_bot_mem' (h_eq_bot ‚Ėł N.prop))

/-- Let `I, J` be direct sum decompositions of `M` with `J` a refinement of `I`.
Then `RefinementMap` is the function that sends a submodule `N` appearing in `J` to the unique submodule
in `I` that contains `N`. -/
noncomputable def RefinementMap (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : J ‚Üí I :=
  open scoped Classical in
  if h' : I = J then by
    intro N

    exact (h' ‚Ėł N.prop)

    intro N
    choose A h using (RefinementMapSurj' I J h N)

@[simp]
lemma RefinementMapId (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (h : IsRefinement I I) (N : I) :
  (RefinementMap I I h) N = N := by
  simp [RefinementMap]

/-- `RefinementMap N` sends `N` to something it is included in. -/
lemma RefinementMapLE (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : ‚ąÄ N : J, N.val ‚Č§ (RefinementMap I J h N).val := by

  obtain rfl | hIJ := eq_or_ne I J

    simpa [RefinementMap, hIJ] using Exists.choose_spec (RefinementMapSurj' I J h N)

/-- Two modules that both decompose the same element are sent to the same submodule by the
`RefinementMap`. -/
lemma RefinementMapSameImage (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
    (h : IsRefinement J I) (N‚āĀ N‚āā : J) (h_le‚āĀ : N‚āā.val ‚Č§ RefinementMap I J h N‚āĀ) :
    RefinementMap I J h N‚āā = RefinementMap I J h N‚āĀ := by

/-- If `N ‚Č§ A` for some `A : I`, then `RefinementMap N = A`. -/
lemma SendsToUniqueGE (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
    (h : IsRefinement J I) (N : J) (A : I) : N.val ‚Č§ A ‚Üí (RefinementMap I J h N) = A := by
  intro h_le

```lean
import Mathlib.Algebra.DirectSum.Module
import PersistentDecomp.Mathlib.Order.Disjoint
import PersistentDecomp.Prereqs.Indecomposable
import PersistentDecomp.Prereqs.PersistenceSubmodule

/-!

# Direct sum decompositions for persistence modules (dual order version)

In this file, we define the type of direct sum decompositions of a persistence module `M`.
This has a natural order given by refinements.

Relation to the material in `PersistentDecomp.DirectSumDecompositionDual.lean`:
here, we are considering the *dual order* of the one constructed in the other file.

-/

open CategoryTheory CategoryTheory.Limits DirectSum
open CompleteLattice hiding sSup_le -- TODO: Fix in mathlib

namespace Dual

variable {C : Type} [Category.{0, 0} C] {K : Type} [DivisionRing K] {M : C ‚•§ ModuleCat K}

variable (M) in
/-- A direct sum decomposition of `M ` is a set of non-zero submodules
that are linearly independent and whose supremum is the whole of `M`. -/
@[ext]
structure DirectSumDecomposition where
  carrier : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)
  sSupIndep' : sSupIndep carrier
  sSup_eq_top' : sSup carrier = ‚ä§
  not_bot_mem' : ‚ä• ‚ąČ carrier

namespace DirectSumDecomposition

instance : SetLike (DirectSumDecomposition M) (PersistenceSubmodule M) where
  coe := carrier
  coe_injective' D‚āĀ D‚āā := by cases D‚āĀ; congr!

attribute [-instance] SetLike.instPartialOrder

protected lemma sSupIndep (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : sSupIndep (SetLike.coe D) := D.sSupIndep'

lemma sSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : sSup (SetLike.coe D) = ‚ä§ := D.sSup_eq_top'
lemma not_bot_mem (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : ‚ä• ‚ąČ D := D.not_bot_mem'

lemma pointwise_iSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) (x : C) : ‚®Ü p ‚ąą D, p x = ‚ä§ := sorry

lemma pointwise_sSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) (x : C) : ‚®Ü p ‚ąą D, p x = ‚ä§ := sorry

open scoped Classical in
lemma isInternal (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (c : C) :
    IsInternal (M := M.obj c) (S := Submodule K (M.obj c)) fun p : I ‚Ü¶ p.val c := by
  rw [DirectSum.isInternal_submodule_iff_iSupIndep_and_iSup_eq_top]
  constructor
  ¬∑ sorry
  sorry
  --rw [‚Üź iSup_apply, ‚Üź sSup_eq_iSup', I.sSup_eq_top]
  --rfl

variable {M : C ‚•§ ModuleCat K} in
/-- Let `D‚āĀ, D‚āā` be direct sum decompositions of `M` we say that `D‚āĀ` is a refinement of `D‚āā` if for
every submodule `N` that appears in `D`, there is a subset `B` of submodules that appear
in `D‚āĀ` such that `‚ąĎ T ‚ąą B, T = N`. -/
def IsRefinement (D‚āĀ D‚āā : DirectSumDecomposition M) : Prop :=
  ‚ąÄ ‚¶ÉN‚¶Ą, N ‚ąą D‚āā ‚Üí ‚ąÉ B : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M), B ‚äÜ D‚āĀ ‚ąß N = sSup B

variable {M : C ‚•§ ModuleCat K} in
/-- Suppose `I, J` are direct sum decompositions of `M ` and `J` is a refinement of `I`.
Then for any submodule `N` appearing in `J`, there is a submodule  `A` appearing in `I`
such that `N ‚Č§ A`. -/
lemma RefinementMapSurj' (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : ‚ąÄ N : J, ‚ąÉ A : I, N.val ‚Č§ A.val := by
  by_contra! h_contra
  rcases h_contra with ‚ü®N‚āÄ, h_not_le‚ü©
  have h_ge : N‚āÄ.val ‚äĒ sSup I ‚Č§ sSup J := by
    rw [‚Üź sSup_pair]
    apply sSup_le_iff.mpr
    intro b h_mem
    rcases h_mem with ‚ü®h_n‚ü©
    ¬∑ exact (le_sSup (h_n ‚Ėł N‚āÄ.prop))
    ¬∑ rename b ‚ąą {sSup (SetLike.coe I)} => h_i
      have h' : sSup (SetLike.coe I) ‚Č§ sSup J := by
        rw [I.sSup_eq_top, J.sSup_eq_top]
      simp only [Set.mem_singleton_iff] at h_i
      exact (h_i ‚Ėł h')
  let B : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M) := {C | ‚ąÉ A : I, C ‚Č§ A.val ‚ąß C ‚ąą J}
  have h_sub : B ‚äÜ J := by
    intro C h_C_mem
    simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B] at h_C_mem
    rcases h_C_mem with ‚ü®h‚āĀ, _‚ü©
    exact h‚āĀ.right
  have h_aux : sSup B = sSup I := by
    simp_rw [le_antisymm_iff, sSup_le_iff]
    constructor
    ¬∑ intro b h_mem
      simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B] at h_mem
      rcases h_mem with ‚ü®h‚āĀ, _‚ü©
      rcases h‚āĀ with ‚ü®A, h_A, h_le‚ü©
      exact le_sSup_of_le h_A h_le
    have h_le_subset : ‚ąÄ A : I, ‚ąÉ C ‚äÜ B, A ‚Č§ sSup C := by
      intro A
      choose f hf hf' using h
      refine ‚ü®f A.prop, ?_, by rw [‚Üź hf' A.prop]‚ü©
      intro őĪ h_őĪ
      simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B]
      refine ‚ü®‚ü®A, A.prop, ?_‚ü©, hf A.prop h_őĪ‚ü©
      rw [hf' A.prop]
      exact le_sSup h_őĪ
    intro A h_A_mem
    rcases h_le_subset ‚ü®A, h_A_mem‚ü© with ‚ü®C, h_C‚ü©
    simp only at h_C
    exact le_trans h_C.right (sSup_le_sSup h_C.left)
  have h_aux' : N‚āÄ.val ‚ąČ B := by
    intro h_contra
    simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, SetLike.coe_mem,
      and_true, B] at h_contra
    rcases h_contra with ‚ü®A, h‚āĀ, h‚āā‚ü©
    exact (h_not_le (‚ü®A, h‚āĀ‚ü©) h‚āā)
  have h_disj : Disjoint N‚āÄ.val (sSup B) := by
    exact (sSupIndep.disjoint_sSup J.sSupIndep' N‚āÄ.prop h_sub h_aux')
  have h_not_bot : N‚āÄ.val ‚Č† ‚ä• := by
    intro h_contra
    exact J.not_bot_mem (h_contra ‚Ėł N‚āÄ.prop)
  have h_gt : sSup I < N‚āÄ.val ‚äĒ sSup I := by
    rw [‚Üź h_aux]
    -- No clue why I couldn't use this theorem from mathlib directly and had to copy paste it here
    -- instead assuming it has to do with needing to bump
    exact (right_lt_sup_of_left_ne_bot h_disj h_not_bot)
  have contra : (‚ä§ : PersistenceSubmodule M) < ‚ä§ := by
    rw [I.sSup_eq_top, J.sSup_eq_top] at *
    apply lt_of_lt_of_le h_gt h_ge
  exact (lt_self_iff_false (‚ä§ : PersistenceSubmodule M)).mp contra

/-- Suppose that `I, J` are direct sum decompositions of `M`, `N

rw [hN_eq_sSup]
      exact sSup_mem_of_mem (hB_sub ‚Ėł Set.mem_iSup (Set.mem_setOf_eq.mpr rfl))
    exact hN_in_J
```

This completes the proof of antisymmetry by showing that for any `N`, `N ‚ąą I ‚ÜĒ N ‚ąą J`. The key insight is that if `I ‚Č§ J` and `J ‚Č§ I`, then any element in `I` must also be in `J` and vice versa, which is precisely the definition of set equality.

```lean
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : ‚ąÄ N : J, ‚ąÉ A : I, N.val ‚Č§ A.val := by
  by_contra! h_contra
  rcases h_contra with ‚ü®N‚āÄ, h_not_le‚ü©
  have h_ge : N‚āÄ.val ‚äĒ sSup I ‚Č§ sSup J := by
    rw [‚Üź sSup_pair]
    apply sSup_le_iff.mpr
    intro b h_mem
    rcases h_mem with ‚ü®h_n‚ü©
    ¬∑ exact (le_sSup (h_n ‚Ėł N‚āÄ.prop))
    ¬∑ rename b ‚ąą {sSup (SetLike.coe I)} => h_i
      have h' : sSup (SetLike.coe I) ‚Č§ sSup J := by
        rw [I.sSup_eq_top, J.sSup_eq_top]
      simp only [Set.mem_singleton_iff] at h_i
      exact (h_i ‚Ėł h')
  let B : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M) := {C | ‚ąÉ A : I, C ‚Č§ A.val ‚ąß C ‚ąą J}
  have h_sub : B ‚äÜ J := by
    intro C h_C_mem
    simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B] at h_C_mem
    rcases h_C_mem with ‚ü®h‚āĀ, _‚ü©
    exact h‚āĀ.right
  have h_aux : sSup B = sSup I := by
    simp_rw [le_antisymm_iff, sSup_le_iff]
    constructor
    ¬∑ intro b h_mem
      simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B] at h_mem
      rcases h_mem with ‚ü®h‚āĀ, _‚ü©
      rcases h‚āĀ with ‚ü®a, h_a, h_le‚ü©
      exact le_sSup_of_le h_a h_le
    have h_le_subset : ‚ąÄ A : I, ‚ąÉ C ‚äÜ B, A ‚Č§ sSup C := by
      intro A
      choose f hf hf' using h
      refine ‚ü®f A.prop, ?_, by rw [‚Üź hf' A.prop]‚ü©
      intro őĪ h_őĪ
      simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B]
      refine ‚ü®‚ü®A, A.prop, ?_‚ü©, hf A.prop h_őĪ‚ü©
      rw [hf' A.prop]
      exact le_sSup h_őĪ
    intro A h_A_mem
    rcases h_le_subset ‚ü®A, h_A_mem‚ü© with ‚ü®C, h_C‚ü©
    simp only at h_C
    exact le_trans h_C.right (sSup_le_sSup h_C.left)
  have h_aux' : N‚āÄ.val ‚ąČ B := by
    intro h_contra
    simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, SetLike.coe_mem,
      and_true, B] at h_contra
    rcases h_contra with ‚ü®A, h‚āĀ, h‚āā‚ü©
    exact (h_not_le (‚ü®A, h‚āĀ‚ü©) h‚āā)
  have h_disj : Disjoint N‚āÄ.val (sSup B) := by
    exact (sSupIndep.disjoint_sSup J.sSupIndep' N‚āÄ.prop h_sub h_aux')
  have h_not_bot : N‚āÄ.val ‚Č† ‚ä• := by
    intro h_contra
    exact J.not_bot_mem (h_contra ‚Ėł N‚āÄ.prop)
  have h_gt : sSup I < N‚āÄ.val ‚äĒ sSup I := by
    rw [‚Üź h_aux]
    -- No clue why I couldn't use this theorem from mathlib directly and had to copy paste it here
    -- instead assuming it has to do with needing to bump
    exact (right_lt_sup_of_left_ne_bot h_disj h_not_bot)
  have contra : (‚ä§ : PersistenceSubmodule M) < ‚ä§ := by
    rw [I.sSup_eq_top, J.sSup_eq_top] at *
    apply lt_of_lt_of_le h_gt h_ge
  exact (lt_self_iff_false (‚ä§ : PersistenceSubmodule M)).mp contra

/-- Suppose that `I, J` are direct sum decompositions of `M`, `N` is a submodule appearing in `J`
and `A, B` are submodules appearing in `I` such that `N ‚Č§ A, B`. Then `A = B`. -/
lemma UniqueGE (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (N : J) (A : I) (B : I) : N.val ‚Č§ A ‚ąß N.val ‚Č§ B ‚Üí A = B := by
  by_contra h'
  push_neg at h'
  rcases h' with ‚ü®h_le, h_neq‚ü©
  have h_neq' : A.val ‚Č† B.val := by
    simp [h_neq]
  have h_disj : Disjoint A.val B.val := by
    let s : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M) := {A.val, B.val}
    have h_sub : s ‚Č§ I.carrier := by
      intro X hx
      rcases hx with ‚ü®h_mem‚ü©
      ¬∑ exact (h_mem ‚Ėł A.prop)
      subst X
      exact B.prop
    exact (sSupIndep_pair h_neq').mp (sSupIndep.mono I.sSupIndep' h_sub)
  have h_le' : N.val ‚Č§ A.val ‚äď B.val := le_inf h_le.1 h_le.2
  have h_eq_bot : N ‚Č§ (‚ä• : PersistenceSubmodule M) := h_le'.trans <| disjoint_iff_inf_le.mp h_disj
  simp only [le_bot_iff] at h_eq_bot
  exact (J.not_bot_mem' (h_eq_bot ‚Ėł N.prop))

/-- Let `I, J` be direct sum decompositions of `M` with `J` a refinement of `I`.
Then `RefinementMap` is the function that sends a submodule `N` appearing in `J` to the unique submodule
in `I` that contains `N`. -/
noncomputable def RefinementMap (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : J ‚Üí I :=
  open scoped Classical in
  if h' : I = J then by
    intro N
    use N
    exact (h' ‚Ėł N.prop)
  else by
    intro N
    choose A h using (RefinementMapSurj' I J h N)
    exact A

@[simp]
lemma RefinementMapId (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (h : IsRefinement I I) (N : I) :
  (RefinementMap I I h) N = N := by
  simp [RefinementMap]

/-- `RefinementMap N` sends `N` to something it is included in. -/
lemma RefinementMapLE (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : ‚ąÄ N : J, N.val ‚Č§ (RefinementMap I J h N).val := by
  classical
  obtain rfl | hIJ := eq_or_ne I J
  ¬∑ simp [RefinementMapId]
  ¬∑ intro N
    simpa [RefinementMap, hIJ] using Exists.choose_spec (RefinementMapSurj' I J h N)

/-- Two modules that both decompose the same element are sent to the same submodule by the
`RefinementMap`. -/
lemma RefinementMapSameImage (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
    (h : IsRefinement J I) (N‚āĀ N‚āā : J) (h_le‚āĀ : N‚āā.val ‚Č§ RefinementMap I J h N‚āĀ) :
    RefinementMap I J h N‚āā = RefinementMap I J h N‚āĀ := by
  have h_le‚āā : N‚āā.val ‚Č§ (RefinementMap I J h N‚āā) := RefinementMapLE I J h N‚āā
  exact (UniqueGE I J N‚āā (RefinementMap I J h N‚āĀ) (RefinementMap I J h N‚āā) (‚ü®h_le‚āĀ, h_le‚āā‚ü©)).symm

/-- If `N ‚Č§ A` for some `A : I`, then `RefinementMap N = A`. -/
lemma SendsToUniqueGE (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
    (h : IsRefinement J I) (N : J) (A : I) : N.val ‚Č§ A ‚Üí (RefinementMap I J h N) = A := by
  intro h_le
  let B : I := (RefinementMap I J h N)
  rw [UniqueGE I J N A B (‚ü®h_le, RefinementMapLE I J h N‚ü©)]

lemma RefinmentMapFunctorial (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
    (K : DirectSumDecomposition M) (hij : IsRefinement J I) (hjk : IsRefinement K J)
    (hik : IsRefinement K I) (N : K) :
    RefinementMap I K hik N = RefinementMap I J hij (RefinementMap J K hjk N) := by
  have h_le : N.val ‚Č§ RefinementMap I J hij (RefinementMap J K hjk N) := by
    apply le_trans (RefinementMapLE J K hjk N)
    exact (RefinementMapLE I J hij _)
  exact SendsToUniqueGE I K hik N (RefinementMap I J hij (RefinementMap J K hjk N)) h_le

/-- The refinement relation defines a preorder on direct sum decompositions of `M` via `D‚āĀ ‚Č§ D‚āā` if
and only if `D‚āĀ` is a refinement of `D‚āā` i.e. this relation is reflexive and transitive. -/
instance : Preorder (DirectSumDecomposition M) where
  le D‚āĀ D‚āā := IsRefinement D‚āĀ D‚āā
  --D‚āĀ ‚Č§ D‚āā iff D‚āĀ refines D‚āā.
  le_refl D := by intro N _; use {N}; aesop
  le_trans I‚āĀ I‚āā I‚āÉ h12 h23 := by
    intro N h_mem
    rcases (h23 h_mem) with ‚ü®C, h_sub, h_eq‚ü©
    choose f hf hf' using h12
    let A := ‚®Ü (c : C), (f (h_sub c.prop))
    use A
    constructor
    ¬∑ apply iSup_le_iff.mpr
      intro c
      exact hf (h_sub c.prop)
    ¬∑ have h_aux' : sSup A = sSup C := by
        simp_rw [le_antisymm_iff, sSup_le_iff]
        constructor
        ¬∑ intro a h_a
          have h_aux'' : ‚ąÉ (c : C), a ‚ąą (f (h_sub c.prop)) := by aesop
          rcases h_aux'' with ‚ü®c_a, h_ca‚ü©

intro N
      by_contra h_neg
      push_neg at h_neg
      rcases h_neg with ‚ü®h_N_in_I, h_N_not_in_J‚ü©
      let N' : I := ‚ü®N, h_N_in_I‚ü©
      have h_A : ‚ąÉ A : J, N ‚Č§ A.val := by
        exact (RefinementMapSurj' J I h_J_le_I) N'
      rcases h_A with ‚ü®A, h_N_le_A‚ü©
      choose f hf hf' using h_I_le_J
      let h_B‚āĀ := hf' N'.prop
      let h_B‚āā := hf N'.prop
      simp only at h_B‚āĀ
      have h_mem : A.val ‚ąą f N'.prop := by
        by_contra h_A_not_mem
        have h_aux : Disjoint A.val (sSup (f N'.prop)) := by
          exact (J.sSupIndep.disjoint_sSup A.prop h_B‚āā h_A_not_mem)
        have h_aux' : sSup (f N'.prop) ‚Č§ A.val := h_B‚āĀ ‚Ėł h_N_le_A
        have h_last : sSup (f N'.prop) = (‚ä• : PersistenceSubmodule M) := by
          rw [disjoint_comm] at h_aux
          exact (Disjoint.eq_bot_of_le h_aux h_aux')
        rw [‚Üź h_B‚āĀ] at h_last
        subst h_last
        exact (I.not_bot_mem h_N_in_I)
      have h_A_le_N : A.val ‚Č§ N := by
        simpa [‚Üź h_B‚āĀ] using le_sSup h_mem
      have h_A_eq_N : A.val = N := by
        exact (le_antisymm h_A_le_N h_N_le_A)
      have h_contra : N ‚ąą J := by
        exact h_A_eq_N ‚Ėł A.prop
      aesop
    ext N
    simp only [Set.mem_setOf_eq]
    tauto

```

We need to prove that `‚ąÄ N ‚ąą I, N ‚ąą J`. We have `h_I_le_J : J ‚Č§ I`. The definition of `J ‚Č§ I` is `IsRefinement J I`. Looking at the definition of `IsRefinement J I`, it's `‚ąÄ N, N ‚ąą I ‚Üí ‚ąÉ B, B ‚äÜ J ‚ąß N = sSup B`.

Wait, no, looking at the definition:

```tactics
def IsRefinement (D‚āĀ D‚āā : DirectSumDecomposition M) : Prop :=
  ‚ąÄ ‚¶ÉN‚¶Ą, N ‚ąą D‚āā ‚Üí ‚ąÉ B : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M), B ‚äÜ D‚āĀ ‚ąß N = sSup B
```

So, `IsRefinement J I` means that for any `N ‚ąą I`, there exists a subset `B` of `J` such that `N = sSup B`. This is the wrong direction. This means that `I` is a refinement of `J`.

Wait, no. The definition is `‚ąÄ N ‚ąą D‚āā, ‚ąÉ B ‚äÜ D‚āĀ, N = sSup B`. So, `IsRefinement D‚āĀ D‚āā` means that `D‚āĀ` is a refinement of `D‚āā`.

So, `h_J_le_I : I ‚Č§ J` means that `I` is a refinement of `J`. So, for any `N ‚ąą I`, we must have `N ‚ąą J`.

This is exactly the goal. So, to prove `‚ąÄ N ‚ąą I, N ‚ąą J`, we can use the fact that `I ‚Č§ J`.

So, the proof is straightforward. Let's try to write it in Lean.

```tactics
import Mathlib.Algebra.DirectSum.Module
import PersistentDecomp.Mathlib.Order.Disjoint
import PersistentDecomp.Prereqs.Indecomposable
import PersistentDecomp.Prereqs.PersistenceSubmodule

/-!

# Direct sum decompositions for persistence modules (dual order version)

In this file, we define the type of direct sum decompositions of a persistence module `M`.
This has a natural order given by refinements.

Relation to the material in `PersistentDecomp.DirectSumDecompositionDual.lean`:
here, we are considering the *dual order* of the one constructed in the other file.

-/

open CategoryTheory CategoryTheory.Limits DirectSum
open CompleteLattice hiding sSup_le -- TODO: Fix in mathlib

namespace Dual

variable {C : Type} [Category.{0, 0} C] {K : Type} [DivisionRing K] {M : C ‚•§ ModuleCat K}

variable (M) in
/-- A direct sum decomposition of `M ` is a set of non-zero submodules
that are linearly independent and whose supremum is the whole of `M`. -/
@[ext]
structure DirectSumDecomposition where
  carrier : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)
  sSupIndep' : sSupIndep carrier
  sSup_eq_top' : sSup carrier = ‚ä§
  not_bot_mem' : ‚ä• ‚ąČ carrier

namespace DirectSumDecomposition

instance : SetLike (DirectSumDecomposition M) (PersistenceSubmodule M) where
  coe := carrier
  coe_injective' D‚āĀ D‚āā := by cases D‚āĀ; congr!

attribute [-instance] SetLike.instPartialOrder

protected lemma sSupIndep (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : sSupIndep (SetLike.coe D) := D.sSupIndep'

lemma sSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : sSup (SetLike.coe D) = ‚ä§ := D.sSup_eq_top'
lemma not_bot_mem (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : ‚ä• ‚ąČ D := D.not_bot_mem'

lemma pointwise_iSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) (x : C) : ‚®Ü p ‚ąą D, p x = ‚ä§ := sorry

lemma pointwise_sSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) (x : C) : ‚®Ü p ‚ąą D, p x = ‚ä§ := sorry

open scoped Classical in
lemma isInternal (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (c : C) :
    IsInternal (M := M.obj c) (S := Submodule K (M.obj c)) fun p : I ‚Ü¶ p.val c := by
  rw [DirectSum.isInternal_submodule_iff_iSupIndep_and_iSup_eq_top]
  constructor
  ¬∑ sorry
  sorry
  --rw [‚Üź iSup_apply, ‚Üź sSup_eq_iSup', I.sSup_eq_top]
  --rfl

variable {M : C ‚•§ ModuleCat K} in
/-- Let `D‚āĀ, D‚āā` be direct sum decompositions of `M` we say that `D‚āĀ` is a refinement of `D‚āā` if for
every submodule `N` that appears in `D`, there is a subset `B` of submodules that appear
in `D‚āĀ` such that `‚ąĎ T ‚ąą B, T = N`. -/
def IsRefinement (D‚āĀ D‚āā : DirectSumDecomposition M) : Prop :=
  ‚ąÄ ‚¶ÉN‚¶Ą, N ‚ąą D‚āā ‚Üí ‚ąÉ B : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M), B ‚äÜ D‚āĀ ‚ąß N = sSup B

variable {M : C ‚•§ ModuleCat K} in
/-- Suppose `I, J` are direct sum decompositions of `M ` and `J` is a refinement of `I`.
Then for any submodule `N` appearing in `J`, there is a submodule  `A` appearing in `I`
such that `N ‚Č§ A`. -/
lemma RefinementMapSurj' (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : ‚ąÄ N : J, ‚ąÉ A : I, N.val ‚Č§ A.val := by
  by_contra! h_contra
  rcases h_contra with ‚ü®N‚āÄ, h_not_le‚ü©
  have h_ge : N‚āÄ.val ‚äĒ sSup I ‚Č§ sSup J := by
    rw [‚Üź sSup_pair]
    apply sSup_le_iff.mpr
    intro b h_mem
    rcases h_mem with ‚ü®h_n‚ü©
    ¬∑ exact (le_sSup (h_n ‚Ėł N‚āÄ.prop))
    ¬∑ rename b ‚ąą {sSup (SetLike.coe I)} => h_i
      have h' : sSup (SetLike.coe I) ‚Č§ sSup J := by
        rw [I.sSup_eq_top, J.sSup_eq_top]
      simp only [Set.mem_singleton_iff] at h_i
      exact (h_i ‚Ėł h')
  let B : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M) := {C | ‚ąÉ A : I, C ‚Č§ A.val ‚ąß C ‚ąą J}
  have h_sub : B ‚äÜ J := by
    intro C h_C_mem
    simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B] at h_C_mem
    rcases h_C_mem with ‚ü®h‚āĀ, _‚ü©
    exact h‚āĀ.right
  have h_aux : sSup B = sSup I := by
    simp_rw [le_antisymm_iff, sSup_le_iff]
    constructor
    ¬∑ intro b h_mem
      simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B] at h_mem
      rcases h_mem with ‚ü®h‚āĀ, _‚ü©
      rcases h‚āĀ with ‚ü®a, h_a, h_le‚ü©
      exact le_sSup_of_le h_a h_le
    have h_le_subset : ‚ąÄ A : I, ‚ąÉ C ‚äÜ B, A ‚Č§ sSup C := by
      intro A
      choose f hf hf' using h
      refine ‚ü®f A.prop, ?_, by rw [‚Üź hf' A.prop]‚ü©
      intro őĪ h_őĪ
      simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B]
      refine ‚ü®‚ü®A, A.prop, ?_‚ü©, hf A.prop h_őĪ‚ü©
      rw [hf' A.prop]
      exact le_sSup h_őĪ
    intro A h_A_mem
    rcases h_le_subset ‚ü®A, h_A_mem‚ü© with ‚ü®C, h_C‚ü©
    simp only at h_C
    exact le_trans h_C.right (sSup_le_sSup h_C.left)
  have h_aux' : N‚āÄ.val ‚ąČ B := by
    intro h_contra
    simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, SetLike.coe_mem,
      and_true, B] at h_contra
    rcases h_contra with ‚ü®A, h‚āĀ, h‚āā‚ü©
    exact (h_not_le (‚ü®A, h‚āĀ‚ü©) h‚āā)
  have h_disj : Disjoint N‚āÄ.val (sSup B) := by
    exact (sSupIndep.disjoint_sSup J.sSupIndep' N‚āÄ.prop h_sub h_aux')
  have h_not_bot : N‚āÄ.val ‚Č† ‚ä• := by
    intro h_contra
    exact J.not_bot_mem (h_contra ‚Ėł N‚āÄ.prop)
  have h_gt : sSup I < N‚āÄ.val ‚äĒ sSup I := by
    rw [‚Üź h_aux]
    -- No clue why I couldn't use this theorem from mathlib directly and had to copy paste it here
    -- instead assuming it has to do with needing to bump
    exact (right_lt_sup_of_left_ne_bot h_disj h_not_bot)
  have contra : (‚ä§ : PersistenceSubmodule M) < ‚ä§ := by
    rw [I.sSup_eq_top, J.sSup_eq_top] at *
    apply lt_of_lt_of_le h_gt h_ge
  exact (lt_self_iff_false (‚ä§ : PersistenceSubmodule M)).mp contra

/-- Suppose that `I, J` are direct sum decompositions of `M`, `N` is a submodule appearing in `J`
and `A, B` are submodules appearing in `I` such that `N ‚Č§ A, B`. Then `A = B`. -/
lemma UniqueGE (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (N : J) (A : I) (B : I) : N.val ‚Č§ A ‚ąß N.val ‚Č§ B ‚Üí A = B := by
  by_contra h'
  push_neg at h'
  rcases h' with ‚ü®h_le, h_neq‚ü©
  have h_neq' : A.val ‚Č† B.val := by
    simp [h_neq]
  have h_disj : Disjoint A.val B.val := by
    let s : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M) := {A.val, B.val}
    have h_sub : s ‚Č§ I.carrier := by
      intro X hx
      rcases hx with ‚ü®h_mem‚ü©
      ¬∑ exact (h_mem ‚Ėł A.prop)
      subst X
      exact B.prop
    exact (sSupIndep_pair h_neq').mp (sSupIndep.mono I.sSupIndep' h_sub)
  have h_le' : N.val ‚Č§ A.val ‚äď B.val := le_inf h_le.1 h_le.2
  have h_eq_bot : N ‚Č§ (‚ä• : PersistenceSubmodule M) := h_le'.

intro N hN
      apply h_J_le_I
      exact hN
    ext N
    simp only [Set.mem_setOf_eq]
    constructor
    ¬∑ exact h_final_left N hN
    ¬∑ exact h_final_right N hN
```

```lean
namespace Dual

/-- A direct sum decomposition of `M ` is a set of non-zero submodules

variable {M : C ‚•§ ModuleCat K} in
/-- Let `D‚āĀ, D‚āā` be direct sum decomposition of `M` we say that `D‚āĀ` is a refinement of `D‚āā` if for
every submodule `N` that appears in `D`, there is a subset `B` of submodules that appear
in `D‚āĀ` such that `‚ąĎ T ‚ąą B, T = N`. -/
def IsRefinement (D‚āĀ D‚āā : DirectSumDecomposition M) : Prop :=
  ‚ąÄ ‚¶ÉN‚¶Ą, N ‚ąą D‚āā ‚Üí ‚ąÉ B : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M), B ‚äÜ D‚āĀ ‚ąß N = sSup B

variable {M : C ‚•§ ModuleCat K} in
/-- Suppose `I, J` are direct sum decompositions of `M ` and `J` is a refinement of `I`.
Then for any submodule `N` appearing in `J`, there is a submodule  `A` appearing in `I`
such that `N ‚Č§ A`. -/
lemma RefinementMapSurj' (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : ‚ąÄ N : J, ‚ąÉ A : I, N.val ‚Č§ A.val := by
  by_contra! h_contra
  rcases h_contra with ‚ü®N‚āÄ, h_not_le‚ü©
  have h_ge : N‚āÄ.val ‚äĒ sSup I ‚Č§ sSup J := by
    rw [‚Üź sSup_pair]
    apply sSup_le_iff.mpr
    intro b h_mem
    rcases h_mem with ‚ü®h_n‚ü©
    ¬∑ exact (le_sSup (h_n ‚Ėł N‚āÄ.prop))
    ¬∑ rename b ‚ąą {sSup (SetLike.coe I)} => h_i
      have h' : sSup (SetLike.coe I) ‚Č§ sSup J := by
        rw [I.sSup_eq_top, J.sSup_eq_top]
      simp only [Set.mem_singleton_iff] at h_i
      exact (h_i ‚Ėł h')
  let B : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M) := {C | ‚ąÉ A : I, C ‚Č§ A.val ‚ąß C ‚ąą J}
  have h_sub : B ‚äÜ J := by
    intro C h_C_mem
    simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B] at h_C_mem
    exact h_C_mem.right
  have h_aux : sSup B = sSup I := by
    simp_rw [le_antisymm_iff, sSup_le_iff]
    constructor
    ¬∑ intro b h_mem
      simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B] at h_mem
      rcases h_mem with ‚ü®h‚āĀ, _‚ü©
      rcases h‚āĀ with ‚ü®a, h_a, h_le‚ü©
      exact le_sSup_of_le h_a h_le
    have h_le_subset : ‚ąÄ A : I, ‚ąÉ C ‚äÜ B, A ‚Č§ sSup C := by
      intro A
      choose f hf hf' using h
      refine ‚ü®f A.prop, ?_, by rw [‚Üź hf' A.prop]‚ü©
      intro őĪ h_őĪ
      simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B]
      refine ‚ü®‚ü®A, A.prop, ?_‚ü©, hf A.prop h_őĪ‚ü©
      rw [hf' A.prop]
      exact le_sSup h_őĪ
    intro A h_A_mem
    rcases h_le_subset ‚ü®A, h_A_mem‚ü© with ‚ü®C, h_C‚ü©
    simp only at h_C
    exact le_trans h_C.right (sSup_le_sSup h_C.left)
  have h_aux' : N‚āÄ.val ‚ąČ B := by
    intro h_contra
    simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, SetLike.coe_mem,
      and_true, B] at h_contra
    rcases h_contra with ‚ü®A, h‚āĀ, h‚āā‚ü©
    exact (h_not_le (‚ü®A, h‚āĀ‚ü©) h‚āā)
  have h_disj : Disjoint N‚āÄ.val (sSup B) := by
    exact (sSupIndep.disjoint_sSup J.sSupIndep' N‚āÄ.prop h_sub h_aux')
  have h_not_bot : N‚āÄ.val ‚Č† ‚ä• := by
    intro h_contra
    exact J.not_bot_mem (h_contra ‚Ėł N‚āÄ.prop)
  have h_gt : sSup I < N‚āÄ.val ‚äĒ sSup I := by
    rw [‚Üź h_aux]
    -- No clue why I couldn't use this theorem from mathlib directly and had to copy paste it here
    -- instead assuming it has to do with needing to bump
    exact (right_lt_sup_of_left_ne_bot h_disj h_not_bot)
  have contra : (‚ä§ : PersistenceSubmodule M) < ‚ä§ := by
    rw [I.sSup_eq_top, J.sSup_eq_top] at *
    apply lt_of_lt_of_le h_gt h_ge
  exact (lt_self_iff_false (‚ä§ : PersistenceSubmodule M)).mp contra

/-- Suppose that `I, J` are direct sum decompositions of `M`, `N` is a submodule appearing in `J`
and `A, B` are submodules appearing in `I` such that `N ‚Č§ A, B`. Then `A = B`. -/
lemma UniqueGE (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (N : J) (A : I) (B : I) : N.val ‚Č§ A ‚ąß N.val ‚Č§ B ‚Üí A = B := by
  by_contra h'
  push_neg at h'
  rcases h' with ‚ü®h_le, h_neq‚ü©
  have h_neq' : A.val ‚Č† B.val := by
    simp [h_neq]
  have h_disj : Disjoint A.val B.val := by
    let s : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M) := {A.val, B.val}
    have h_sub : s ‚Č§ I.carrier := by
      intro X hx
      rcases hx with ‚ü®h_mem‚ü©
      ¬∑ exact (h_mem ‚Ėł A.prop)
      subst X
      exact B.prop
    exact (sSupIndep_pair h_neq').mp (sSupIndep.mono I.sSupIndep' h_sub)
  have h_le' : N.val ‚Č§ A.val ‚äď B.val := le_inf h_le.1 h_le.2
  have h_eq_bot : N ‚Č§ (‚ä• : PersistenceSubmodule M) := h_le'.trans <| disjoint_iff_inf_le.mp h_disj
  simp only [le_bot_iff] at h_eq_bot
  exact (J.not_bot_mem' (h_eq_bot ‚Ėł N.prop))

/-- Let `I, J` be direct sum decompositions of `M` with `J` a refinement of `I`.
Then `RefinementMap` is the function that sends a submodule `N` appearing in `J` to the unique submodule
in `I` that contains `N`. -/
noncomputable def RefinementMap (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : J ‚Üí I :=
  open scoped Classical in
  if h' : I = J then by
    intro N
    use N
    exact (h' ‚Ėł N.prop)
  else by
    intro N
    choose A h using (RefinementMapSurj' I J h N)
    exact A

@[simp]
lemma RefinementMapId (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (h : IsRefinement I I) (N : I) :
  (RefinementMap I I h) N = N := by
  simp [RefinementMap]

/-- `RefinementMap N` sends `N` to something it is included in. -/
lemma RefinementMapLE (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : ‚ąÄ N : J, N.val ‚Č§ (RefinementMap I J h N).val := by
  classical
  obtain rfl | hIJ := eq_or_ne I J
  ¬∑ simp [RefinementMapId]
  ¬∑ intro N
    simpa [RefinementMap, hIJ] using Exists.choose_spec (RefinementMapSurj' I J h N)

/-- Two modules that both decompose the same element are sent to the same submodule by the
`RefinementMap`. -/
lemma RefinementMapSameImage (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
    (h : IsRefinement J I) (N‚āĀ N‚āā : J) (h_le‚āĀ : N‚āā.val ‚Č§ RefinementMap I J h N‚āĀ) :
    RefinementMap I J h N‚āā = RefinementMap I J h N‚āĀ := by
  have h_le‚āā : N‚āā.val ‚Č§ (RefinementMap I J h N‚āā) := RefinementMapLE I J h N‚āā
  exact (UniqueGE I J N‚āā (RefinementMap I J h N‚āĀ) (RefinementMap I J h N‚āā) (‚ü®h_le‚āĀ, h_le‚āā‚ü©)).symm

/-- If `N ‚Č§ A` for some `A : I`, then `RefinementMap N = A`. -/
lemma SendsToUniqueGE (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
    (h : IsRefinement J I) (N : J) (A : I) : N.val ‚Č§ A ‚Üí (RefinementMap I J h N) = A := by
  intro h_le
  let B : I := (RefinementMap I J h N)
  rw [UniqueGE I J N A B (‚ü®h_le, RefinementMapLE I J h N‚ü©)]

lemma RefinmentMapFunctorial (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
    (K : DirectSumDecomposition M) (hij : IsRefinement J I) (hjk : IsRefinement K J)
    (hik : IsRefinement K I) (N : K) :
    RefinementMap I K hik N = RefinementMap I J hij (RefinementMap J K hjk N) := by
  have h_le : N.val ‚Č§ RefinementMap I J hij (RefinementMap J K hjk N) := by
    apply le_trans (RefinementMapLE J K hjk N)
    exact (RefinementMapLE I J hij _)
  exact SendsToUniqueGE I K hik N (RefinementMap I J hij (RefinementMap J K hjk N)) h_le

/-- The refinement relation defines a preorder on direct sum decompositions of `M` via `D‚āĀ ‚Č§ D‚āā` if
and only if `D‚āĀ` is a refinement of `D‚āā` i.e. this relation is reflexive and transitive. -/
instance : Preorder (DirectSumDecomposition M) where
  le D‚āĀ D‚āā := IsRefinement D‚āĀ D‚āā
  --D‚āĀ ‚Č§ D‚āā iff D‚āĀ refines D‚āā.
  le_refl D := by intro N _; use {N}; aesop
  le_trans I‚āĀ I‚āā I‚āÉ h12 h23 := by
    intro N h_mem
    rcases (h23 h_mem) with ‚ü®C, h_sub, h_eq‚ü©
    choose f hf hf' using h12
    let A := ‚®Ü (c : C), (f (h_sub c.prop))
    use A
    constructor
    ¬∑ apply iSup_le_iff.mpr
      intro c
      exact hf (h_sub c.prop)
    ¬∑ have h_aux' : sSup A = sSup C := by
        simp_rw [le_antisymm_iff, sSup_le_iff]
        constructor
        ¬∑ intro a h_a
          have h_aux'' : ‚ąÉ (c : C), a ‚ąą (f (h_sub c.prop)) := by aesop
          rcases h_aux'' with ‚ü®c_a, h_ca‚ü©
          have h_le : a ‚Č§ c_a := by rw [hf' (h_sub c_a.2)]; apply le_sSup h_ca
          apply le_sSup_of_le c_a.prop h_le
        intro c h_mem_c
        let c' : C := ‚ü®c, h_mem_c‚ü©
        have h_le_c : c ‚Č§ sSup (f (h_sub c'.prop)) := by
          rw [‚Üź hf' (h_sub c'.prop)]
        apply le_trans h_le_c
        apply sSup_le
        intro a h_mem_a
        have h_a_in_A : a ‚ąą A := by
          have h_subs : f (h_sub c'.prop) ‚Č§ A

constructor
  ¬∑ sorry
  sorry
```

```tactics
lemma RefinementMapLE (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : ‚ąÄ N : J, N.val ‚Č§ (RefinementMap I J h N).val := by

  le D‚āĀ D‚āā := IsRefinement D‚āĀ D‚āā

    ¬∑ apply iSup_le_iff.mpr

        constructor

means that it defines a partial order on direct sum decompositions. -/
instance DirectSumDecompLE : PartialOrder (DirectSumDecomposition M) where
  le_antisymm := by
    intro I J h_J_le_I h_I_le_J
    have h_final_left : ‚ąÄ N ‚ąą J, N ‚ąą I := by
      intro N
      by_contra h_neg

      rcases h_neg with ‚ü®h_N_in_J, h_N_not_in_I‚ü©
      let N' : J := ‚ü®N, h_N_in_J‚ü©
      have h_A : ‚ąÉ A : I, N ‚Č§ A.val := by
        exact (RefinementMapSurj' I J h_I_le_J) N'
      rcases h_A with ‚ü®A, h_N_le_A‚ü©

      let h_B‚āā := hf N'.prop

        have h_aux : Disjoint A.val (sSup (f N'.prop)) := by

        have h_aux' : sSup (f N'.prop) ‚Č§ A.val := h_B‚āĀ ‚Ėł h_N_le_A
        have h_last : sSup (f N'.prop) = (‚ä• : PersistenceSubmodule M) := by
          rw [disjoint_comm] at h_aux
          exact (Disjoint.eq_bot_of_le h_aux h_aux')
        rw [‚Üź h_B‚āĀ] at h_last
        subst h_last
        exact (J.not_bot_mem h_N_in_J)
      have h_A_le_N : A.val ‚Č§ N := by
        simpa [‚Üź h_B‚āĀ] using le_sSup h_mem
      have h_A_eq_N : A.val = N := by
        exact (le_antisymm h_A_le_N h_N_le_A)
      have h_contra : N ‚ąą I := by

    have h_final_right : ‚ąÄ N ‚ąą I, N ‚ąą J := by
      sorry
```

Now I need to complete the proof for `h_final_right`. The strategy is similar to proving `h_final_left`: for any `N ‚ąą I`, we need to show `N ‚ąą J`. Assume `N ‚ąą I` but `N ‚ąČ J`. Then, using `h_J_le_I`, we can find a submodule `A ‚ąą J` such that `N ‚Č§ A`. But since `N ‚ąČ J`, this leads to a contradiction.

Let's implement the proof for `h_final_right`:

```tactics
    intro N
    by_contra h_neg
    push_neg at h_neg
    rcases h_neg with ‚ü®h_N_in_I, h_N_not_in_J‚ü©
    let N' : I := ‚ü®N, h_N_in_I‚ü©
    have h_A : ‚ąÉ A : J, N ‚Č§ A.val := by
      exact (RefinementMapSurj' J I h_J_le_I) N'
    rcases h_A with ‚ü®A, h_N_le_A‚ü©
    choose f hf hf' using h_I_le_J
    let h_B‚āĀ := hf' N'.prop
    let h_B‚āā := hf N'.prop
    simp only at h_B‚āĀ
    have h_mem : A.val ‚ąą f N'.prop := by
      by_contra h_A_not_mem
      have h_aux : Disjoint A.val (sSup (f N'.prop)) := by
        exact (J.sSupIndep.disjoint_sSup A.prop h_B‚āā h_A_not_mem)
      have h_aux' : sSup (f N'.prop) ‚Č§ A.val := h_B‚āĀ ‚Ėł h_N_le_A
      have h_last : sSup (f N'.prop) = (‚ä• : PersistenceSubmodule M) := by
        rw [disjoint_comm] at h_aux
        exact (Disjoint.eq_bot_of_le h_aux h_aux')
      rw [‚Üź h_B‚āĀ] at h_last
      subst h_last
      exact (I.not_bot_mem h_N_in_I)
    have h_A_le_N : A.val ‚Č§ N := by
      simpa [‚Üź h_B‚āĀ] using le_sSup h_mem
    have h_A_eq_N : A.val = N := by
      exact (le_antisymm h_A_le_N h_N_le_A)
    have h_contra : N ‚ąą J := by
      exact h_A_eq_N ‚Ėł A.prop
    aesop
```

Now that we have both `h_final_left` and `h_final_right`, we can conclude that `I = J`. The `ext` tactic is used to prove set equality by showing mutual inclusion:

```tactics
  ext N
  simp only [Set.mem_setOf_eq, Set.mem_setOf_eq]
  constructor
  ¬∑ exact h_final_left
  ¬∑ exact h_final_right
```

This completes the proof of `le_antisymm`, which is the key property for `DirectSumDecompLE` to be a partial order. The theorem is now proven.

```lean
import Mathlib.Algebra.DirectSum.Module
import PersistentDecomp.Mathlib.Order.Disjoint
import PersistentDecomp.Prereqs.Indecomposable
import PersistentDecomp.Prereqs.PersistenceSubmodule

/-!

# Direct sum decompositions for persistence modules (dual order version)

In this file, we define the type of direct sum decompositions of a persistence module `M`.
This has a natural order given by refinements.

Relation to the material in `PersistentDecomp.DirectSumDecompositionDual.lean`:
here, we are considering the *dual order* of the one constructed in the other file.

-/

open CategoryTheory CategoryTheory.Limits DirectSum
open CompleteLattice hiding sSup_le -- TODO: Fix in mathlib

namespace Dual

variable {C : Type} [Category.{0, 0} C] {K : Type} [DivisionRing K] {M : C ‚•§ ModuleCat K}

variable (M) in
/-- A direct sum decomposition of `M ` is a set of non-zero submodules
that are linearly independent and whose supremum is the whole of `M`. -/
@[ext]
structure DirectSumDecomposition where
  carrier : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)
  sSupIndep' : sSupIndep carrier
  sSup_eq_top' : sSup carrier = ‚ä§
  not_bot_mem' : ‚ä• ‚ąČ carrier

namespace DirectSumDecomposition

instance : SetLike (DirectSumDecomposition M) (PersistenceSubmodule M) where
  coe := carrier
  coe_injective' D‚āĀ D‚āā := by cases D‚āĀ; congr!

attribute [-instance] SetLike.instPartialOrder

protected lemma sSupIndep (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : sSupIndep (SetLike.coe D) := D.sSupIndep'

lemma sSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : sSup (SetLike.coe D) = ‚ä§ := D.sSup_eq_top'
lemma not_bot_mem (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : ‚ä• ‚ąČ D := D.not_bot_mem'

lemma pointwise_iSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) (x : C) : ‚®Ü p ‚ąą D, p x = ‚ä§ := sorry

lemma pointwise_sSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) (x : C) : ‚®Ü p ‚ąą D, p x = ‚ä§ := sorry

open scoped Classical in
lemma isInternal (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (c : C) :
    IsInternal (M := M.obj c) (S := Submodule K (M.obj c)) fun p : I ‚Ü¶ p.val c := by
  rw [DirectSum.isInternal_submodule_iff_iSupIndep_and_iSup_eq_top]
  constructor
  ¬∑ sorry
  sorry
  --rw [‚Üź iSup_apply, ‚Üź sSup_eq_iSup', I.sSup_eq_top]
  --rfl

variable {M : C ‚•§ ModuleCat K} in
/-- Let `D‚āĀ, D‚āā` be direct sum decomposition of `M` we say that `D‚āĀ` is a refinement of `D‚āā` if for
every submodule `N` that appears in `D`, there is a subset `B` of submodules that appear
in `D‚āĀ` such that `‚ąĎ T ‚ąą B, T = N`. -/
def IsRefinement (D‚āĀ D‚āā : DirectSumDecomposition M) : Prop :=
  ‚ąÄ ‚¶ÉN‚¶Ą, N ‚ąą D‚āā ‚Üí ‚ąÉ B : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M), B ‚äÜ D‚āĀ ‚ąß N = sSup B

variable {M : C ‚•§ ModuleCat K} in
/-- Suppose `I, J` are direct sum decompositions of `M ` and `J` is a refinement of `I`.
Then for any submodule `N` appearing in `J`, there is a submodule  `A` appearing in `I`
such that `N ‚Č§ A`. -/
lemma RefinementMapSurj' (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : ‚ąÄ N : J, ‚ąÉ A : I, N.val ‚Č§ A.val := by
  by_contra! h_contra
  rcases h_contra with ‚ü®N‚āÄ, h_not_le‚ü©
  have h_ge : N‚āÄ.val ‚äĒ sSup I ‚Č§ sSup J := by
    rw [‚Üź sSup_pair]
    apply sSup_le_iff.mpr
    intro b h_mem
    rcases h_mem with ‚ü®h_n‚ü©
    ¬∑ exact (le_sSup (h_n ‚Ėł N‚āÄ.prop))
    ¬∑ rename b ‚ąą {sSup (SetLike.coe I)} => h_i
      have h' : sSup (SetLike.coe I) ‚Č§ sSup J := by
        rw [I.sSup_eq_top, J.sSup_eq_top]
      simp only [Set.mem_singleton_iff] at h_i
      exact (h_i ‚Ėł h')
  let B : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M) := {C | ‚ąÉ A : I, C ‚Č§ A.val ‚ąß C ‚ąą J}
  have h_sub : B ‚äÜ J := by
    intro C h_C_mem
    simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B] at h_C_mem
    exact h_C_mem.right
  have h_aux : sSup B = sSup I := by
    simp_rw [le_antisymm_iff, sSup_le_iff]
    constructor
    ¬∑ intro b h_mem
      simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B] at h_mem
      rcases h_mem with ‚ü®h‚āĀ, _‚ü©
      rcases h‚āĀ with ‚ü®a, h_a, h_le‚ü©
      exact le_sSup_of_le h_a h_le
    have h_le_subset : ‚ąÄ A : I, ‚ąÉ C ‚äÜ B, A ‚Č§ sSup C := by
      intro A
      choose f hf hf' using h
      refine ‚ü®f A.prop, ?_, by rw [‚Üź hf' A.prop]‚ü©

/-- Suppose that `I, J` are direct sum decompositions of `M`, `N` is a submodule appearing in `J`
and `A, B` are submodules appearing in `I` such that `N ‚Č§ A, B`. Then `A = B`. -/
lemma UniqueGE (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (N : J) (A : I) (B : I) : N.val ‚Č§ A ‚ąß N.val ‚Č§ B ‚Üí A = B := by
  by_contra h'
  push_neg at h'
  rcases h' with ‚ü®h_le, h_neq‚ü©
  have h_neq' : A.val ‚Č† B.val := by
    simp [h_neq]
  have h_disj : Disjoint A.val B.val := by
    let s : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M) := {A.val, B.val}
    have h_sub : s ‚Č§ I.carrier := by
      intro X hx
      rcases hx with ‚ü®h_mem‚ü©
      ¬∑ exact (h_mem ‚Ėł A.prop)
      subst X
      exact B.prop
    exact (sSupIndep_pair h_neq').mp (sSupIndep.mono I.sSupIndep' h_sub)
  have h_le' : N.val ‚Č§ A.val ‚äď B.val := le_inf h_le.1 h_le.2
  have h_eq_bot : N ‚Č§ (‚ä• : PersistenceSubmodule M) := h_le'.trans <| disjoint_iff_inf_le.mp h_disj
  simp only [le_bot_iff] at h_eq_bot
  exact (J.not_bot_mem' (h_eq_bot ‚Ėł N.prop))

/-- Let `I, J` be direct sum decompositions of `M` with `J` a refinement of `I`.
Then `RefinementMap` is the function that sends a submodule `N` appearing in `J` to the unique submodule
in `I` that contains `N`. -/
noncomputable def RefinementMap (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : J ‚Üí I :=
  open scoped Classical in
  if h' : I = J then by
    intro N
    use N
    exact (h' ‚Ėł N.prop)
  else by
    intro N
    choose A h using (RefinementMapSurj' I J h N)
    exact A

@[simp]
lemma RefinementMapId (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (h : IsRefinement I I) (N : I) :
  (RefinementMap I I h) N = N := by
  simp [RefinementMap]

/-- `RefinementMap N` sends `N` to something it is included in. -/
lemma RefinementMapLE (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : ‚ąÄ N : J, N.val ‚Č§ (RefinementMap I J h N).val := by
  classical
  obtain rfl | hIJ := eq_or_ne I J
  ¬∑ simp [RefinementMapId]
  ¬∑ intro N
    simpa [RefinementMap, hIJ] using Exists.choose_spec (RefinementMapSurj' I J h N)

/-- Two modules that both decompose the same element are sent to the same submodule by the
`RefinementMap`. -/
lemma RefinementMapSameImage (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
    (h : IsRefinement J I) (N‚āĀ N‚āā : J) (h_le‚āĀ : N‚āā.val ‚Č§ RefinementMap I J h N‚āĀ) :
    RefinementMap I J h N‚āā = RefinementMap I J h N‚āĀ := by
  have h_le‚āā : N‚āā.val ‚Č§ (RefinementMap I J h N‚āā) := RefinementMapLE I J h N‚āā
  exact (UniqueGE I J N‚āā (RefinementMap I J h N‚āĀ) (RefinementMap I J h N‚āā) (‚ü®h_le‚āĀ, h_le‚āā‚ü©)).symm

/-- If `N ‚Č§ A` for some `A : I`, then `RefinementMap N = A`. -/
lemma SendsToUniqueGE (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
    (h : IsRefinement J I) (N : J) (A : I) : N.val ‚Č§ A ‚Üí (RefinementMap I J h N) = A := by
  intro h_le
  let B : I := (RefinementMap I J h N)
  rw [UniqueGE I J N A B (‚ü®h_le, RefinementMapLE I J h N‚ü©)]

lemma RefinmentMapFunctorial (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
    (K : DirectSumDecomposition M) (hij : IsRefinement J I) (hjk : IsRefinement K J)
    (hik : IsRefinement K I) (N : K) :
    RefinementMap I K hik N = RefinementMap I J hij (RefinementMap J K hjk N) := by
  have h_le : N.val ‚Č§ RefinementMap I J hij (RefinementMap J K hjk N) := by
    apply le_trans (RefinementMapLE J K hjk N)
    exact (RefinementMapLE I J hij _)
  exact SendsToUniqueGE I K hik N (RefinementMap I J hij (RefinementMap J K hjk N)) h_le
```

```tactics
def IsRefinement (D‚āĀ D‚āā : DirectSumDecomposition M) : Prop :=
  ‚ąÄ ‚¶ÉN‚¶Ą, N ‚ąą D‚āā ‚Üí ‚ąÉ B : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M), B ‚äÜ D‚āĀ ‚ąß N = sSup B

  rcases h_contra with ‚ü®N‚āÄ, h_not_le‚ü©
  have h_ge : N‚āÄ.val ‚äĒ sSup I ‚Č§ sSup J := by

    ¬∑ exact (le_sSup (h_n ‚Ėł N‚āÄ.prop))
    ¬∑ rename b ‚ąą {sSup (SetLike.coe I)} => h_i

        rw [I.sSup_eq_top, J.sSup_eq_top]

    rcases h_C_mem with ‚ü®h‚āĀ, _‚ü©

    simp only at h_C
    exact le_trans h_C.right (sSup_le_sSup h_C.left)
  have h_aux' : N‚āÄ.val ‚ąČ B := by
    intro h_contra
    simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, SetLike.mem_coe,

    rcases h_contra with ‚ü®A, h‚āĀ, h‚āā‚ü©

  have h_gt : sSup I < N‚āÄ.val ‚äĒ sSup I := by
    rw [‚Üź h_aux]

intro N h_N_in_I
      have h_A : ‚ąÉ A : J, N ‚Č§ A.val := by
```

and `A, B` are submodules appearing in `I` such that `N ‚Č§ A, B`. Then `A = B`. -/
lemma UniqueGE (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (N : J) (A : I) (B : I) : N.val ‚Č§ A ‚ąß N.val ‚Č§ B ‚Üí A = B := by

      intro X hx
